description: Strict platform-specific guidelines for the Qatar Airways Flow Design System. Check this file before implementing or auditing any platform-specific feature. These rules are non-negotiable — violations break platform integrity.

app.description: Native iOS and Android mobile app components
keyRules: No Viewport prop — App components are inherently mobile-sized
State values include Pressed (not Hover) — hover is a pointer/desktop concept
Bottom Navigation Bar: max 5 tabs, each icon must be ic_nav_menu_ at 32px
Status Bar must always appear above the Navigation Bar in full-screen designs
App Drawers open from the bottom — not from the side (Web uses Sidesheet for side panels)
Modal on App = Modal Alert component only (not Web Modal)
iOS Keyboard and Android Keyboard are separate components — never swap them
Use Handle Bar component at the top of all bottom-sheet drawers
Home Indicator is an iOS hardware element — include it in iPhone frames
OTP Input is App-only — Web uses standard Input Field for OTP flows

App-only components: App Tutorial, Bottom Navigation Bar, Conversational Header, Handle Bar, Home Banner, iOS Notifications, Keyboard (iOS + Android), Main Menu, Membership Cards, Membership Progress Bar, Membership Progress Circles, Modal Alert, OTP Input, Salutations, Search List, Status Bar, Tab Bar

ic_nav_.use: All UI icons — navigation, actions, utilities

ic_nav_menu_.use: Bottom navigation bar tab icons only

forbiddenPatterns: Do not use Web Sidesheet on App — use Drawer (bottom sheet)
Do not use Web Modal on App — use Modal Alert or Drawer
Do not use Web Super Nav — use Navigation Bar (App)
Do not use Web Footer on App — use Bottom Navigation Bar
Do not add a Viewport prop to App components
Do not use Hover state — use Pressed instead
Do not use Web Breadcrumbs on App
Do not use Web Scroll Bar on App
Do not mix iOS and Android keyboard components
Do not create custom bottom navigation — always use Bottom Navigation Bar component

web.description: Desktop, tablet, and mobile web (browser-based) components
keyRules: Always include Viewport prop: Desktop / Tablet / Mobile. Use mWeb for mobile-web-specific responsive variants.
mWeb ≠ Mobile App — mWeb is a browser viewport variant, not a native app screen
Hover and Focus states are required for desktop interactions — do not omit them
Web Sidesheet opens from the right (or left) — not from the bottom (App Drawer does that)
Web Modal is a full overlay dialog — not the same as App Modal Alert
Super Nav is Web-only — the top navigation bar for qatarairways.com
Booking Widget Web has 6 sub-components including Calendar Picker — all must be included in booking flows
Calendar and Calendar Picker are Web-only — App uses Booking Widget with Date Passenger Picker sub-component
Avatar Group is Web-only

Web-only components: 7-day Calendar, Avatar Group, Avios Card, Bench Alert, Booking Autocomplete Dropdown, Booking Wizard, Brand, Breadcrumbs, Calendar, Calendar Picker, Carousel (full), Cart Recovery, Content Module, Counter, Destination Block, Fare Block, Fare Itinerary, Feature Banner, Feedback Banner, Flight Summary Card, Footer, Gallery, Generic Block, Hero Block, Icon Card, Images, Information Card, Interstitials, Lightbox, List Items Dropdown, Loading, Loyalty Login, Marketplace Card, Marketplace Control Card, Membership Card, Modal, Partners Showcase Block, Passenger Details, Passenger Picker, PC Login Pop-up, Progress Tracker, Promotional Banner, Quick Links, Radio Button, Scroll Bar, Section Card, Segment Control, Sidesheet, Slider, Super Nav, Tags
forbiddenPatterns: Do not use App Bottom Navigation Bar on Web — use Super Nav or Footer
Do not use App Status Bar on Web — it is an OS hardware element
Do not use App Home Indicator on Web — iOS hardware only
Do not use App iOS/Android Keyboard on Web — use native browser keyboard
Do not use App Modal Alert on Web — use Web Modal
Do not use App Drawer (bottom sheet) on Web — use Web Sidesheet
Do not omit Viewport prop on Web components
Do not treat mWeb as equivalent to the App platform

email.description: HTML email templates — bilingual (English + Arabic), responsive. 42 components.
keyRules: Email components are a completely separate library — never cross-reference App or Web components
42 email components: headers, bodies, footers, promotional banners, CTAs, informational blocks
Bilingual: every email component has English and Arabic language variants
Responsive: 600px max-width for desktop email clients, single-column stack on mobile
Use table-based layout — not CSS Grid or Flexbox (email client compatibility)
Inline all CSS in production — no external stylesheets
Images: always include alt text, explicit width and height attributes
CTA buttons: bullet-proof VML fallback for Outlook compatibility
Font stack: 'Graphik Web', Arial, sans-serif (Jotia is NOT widely supported in email clients)
No JavaScript in email templates
forbiddenPatterns: Do not use CSS Grid or Flexbox in email markup
Do not use @font-face without solid system font fallbacks
Do not use JavaScript — email clients will strip or block it
Do not use App or Web components in email — email has its own component library
Do not use Jotia for email headings — use Graphik Bold weight instead
Do not use CSS variables (custom properties) in email — not supported by most clients
Do not use SVG inline — use PNG/GIF with ALT text for icons in email

crossPlatformComponents.description: 26 component families exist in both App and Web libraries. These rules govern how they must stay aligned.
alignmentRules: Tier prop values must be identical across platforms: Burgundy, Silver, Gold, Platinum
Direction prop values must be identical: Horizontal, Vertical
Boolean props must use True/False on both platforms — never On/Off
Arabic props must use (Ar) suffix on both platforms — never 'Arabic' prefix
State values may diverge: Web adds Hover and Focus; App uses Pressed instead of Hover
Type values may diverge: Web components often have more variants for desktop contexts
Viewport prop exists on Web only — App components never have Viewport
Naming conflicts to resolve: 'Collapse' (App) → use 'Collapsed' (Web standard); 'In Active' (App) → use 'Inactive'

strictGlobalRules.libraryFirst: If a component exists in the platform library, always use it. Never build a custom equivalent when a library component covers the use case.
strictGlobalRules.iconSourceExclusive: FDS icon libraries (flow-icons-v4) are the ONLY approved icon source. No emoji, no inline SVG files, no third-party icon packs (Heroicons, Material, Feather, etc.).
strictGlobalRules.fontSourceExclusive: FDS font assets (Jotia, Graphik) are the ONLY approved fonts. No Google Fonts, no Adobe Fonts, no CDN substitutes, no Plus Jakarta Sans, no Inter.
strictGlobalRules.tokenRequired: All visual properties (color, spacing, shadow, radius, motion) must reference FDS design tokens. No hardcoded hex, px, or duration values in production code.
strictGlobalRules.arabicRequiresVariant: Structural RTL layout changes must use the Language=Arabic component variant. Manual mirroring, CSS transform: scaleX(-1), or dir=rtl overrides without a proper component variant are forbidden.
strictGlobalRules.noCrossLibraryComponents: App components must not appear in Web designs and vice versa. Email components are always separate.
strictGlobalRules.noDetaching: Library component instances must not be detached. If customisation is needed beyond what props allow, raise an FDS gap request to the design system team.
